leadText: The Sticky Barb (Japanese: くっつきバリ Sticky Barb) is a type of held item introduced in Generation IV. It damages its holder at the end of each turn, but changes holders on contact.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] At the end of the turn, the Sticky Barb damages the holder by 12.5% of its maximum HP each turn. If a Pokémon with no held item hits a Pokémon holding a Sticky Barb with a contact move, including Knock Off , the Sticky Barb will transfer to the Pokémon that used the move (even if the original holder has the Sticky Hold Ability). If the transfer target has a substitute , Sticky Barb will not transfer. The Sticky Barb does not damage Pokémon with the Ability Magic Guard .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt A hold item that damages the holder on every turn. It may latch on to foes that touch the holder. HG SS A held item that damages the holder on every turn. It may latch on to foes that touch the holder. B W B2 W2 A held item that damages the holder on every turn. It may latch on to foes and allies that touch the holder. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA An item to be held by a Pokémon. It damages the holder every turn and may latch on to Pokémon that touch the holder. S V An item to be held by a Pokémon. It damages the holder every turn and may latch on to Pokémon that make direct contact with the holder.
